raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds .txt files in /testbed, counts words, filters those <2000, then compute mean. Use wc -w, awk. Command: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -name '*.txt' -type f -exec wc -w {} + | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1;cnt++} END{print sum/cnt}'. But wc -w outputs filename too. Use awk to skip first field? Actually wc -w outputs "count filename". So awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1;cnt++} END{print sum/cnt}'. That works. Provide command.

task.query: Compute the mean average of the word count of *.txt files smaller than 2000 words in the /testbed directory
task.gold: find /testbed  -name '*.txt' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -w | awk '$1 < 2000 {v += $1; c++} END {print v/c}'
